analysis.mistake: Three keyboard navigation issues in the Header block's dropdown menu: (1) focus outline was animating/blinking instead of staying visible, (2) ArrowDown/Up from the trigger button didn't move focus into dropdown items, (3) ArrowUp/Down inside the dropdown didn't change which item was selected.
analysis.expected: Focus outline should remain stable and visible. ArrowDown/Up from the trigger should move focus into the first/last dropdown item. ArrowUp/Down inside dropdown should cycle through items with visible focus.
analysis.rootCause: Three distinct implementation gaps in the keyboard navigation JavaScript (HeaderMain.js): (1) The outline was using `transition-colors` which was animating the outline-color property, making it blink instead of staying stable; (2) After opening the submenu with `openSubmenu()`, the code immediately tried to focus items but the DOM element still had `display:none` or `hidden` class, so focus() failed silently; (3) The submenu keyboard handler was capturing item references at initialization time rather than re-querying fresh references on each keydown, causing stale DOM references after any DOM manipulation.

suggestions.content: When implementing keyboard navigation with dynamic DOM changes (opening/closing menus, showing/hiding elements): (1) Use `requestAnimationFrame()` after removing `hidden`/`display:none` classes to defer focus operations until the element is actually rendered in the DOM; (2) Re-query DOM elements inside event handlers rather than capturing references at initialization, since the DOM tree may change between when the handler is attached and when the event fires.
suggestions.rationale: Focus management requires elements to be visible in the rendered DOM; transitions and class removals need time to complete before focus can move. Stale DOM references cause keyboard navigation to fail silently.

suggestions.content: When testing keyboard navigation in interactive components (dropdowns, menus, tabs): verify all three aspects: (1) focus outline visibility and stability (not animating or blinking), (2) focus movement from trigger/parent to child items, (3) focus cycling within lists. Test both opening the menu AND navigating to items in a single test sequence. Use `taktician_screenshot` with keyboard actions (e.g., `press('ArrowDown')`, `waitForTimeout`) to verify focus changes visually.
suggestions.rationale: Keyboard navigation has multiple failure modes that won't all be caught by visual inspection alone—focus outline animations, stale element references, and focus trap issues require interactive testing.

suggestions.content: For blocks with keyboard-driven interactions (dropdowns, modals, carousels with keyboard support), add custom QA tests to the qa-plan that specify the exact keyboard sequence to test. Include checks for: (1) focus outline stability across state changes, (2) focus movement from parent to child elements, (3) focus cycling through lists, (4) escape key closing interaction. Reference WAI-ARIA keyboard interaction specs if applicable (e.g., menuitem role requires ArrowUp/Down to cycle items).
suggestions.rationale: Standard QA rules cover structure and visual design but miss keyboard interaction subtleties. Explicit qa-plan entries ensure QA agents run comprehensive keyboard tests, catching focus management bugs before release.
